message:
refactor(abi): switch host-symbol resolution to raw ELF lookup + add startup-readiness guards

Replace versioned `dlvsym_next` lookups with the existing `host_resolve::resolve_host_symbol_raw` resolver across the early-startup paths that were re-entering glibc loader state before `dlerror`/TLS were ready, and gate the remaining vDSO / env / libio-exit paths on `runtime_policy::is_runtime_ready` so they no-op until the membrane pipeline has finished initializing.

Why: calls into host `dlvsym`/`dlsym` from the interposed library can crash during loader startup because glibc's TLS, dlerror state, and `_IO_list_all` have not yet been wired up. Raw-ELF resolution side-steps the loader entirely; the readiness gates avoid touching mutex-backed state from re-entrant init paths (LD_PRELOAD bootstrap, malloc reentry, pipeline init, threading-policy, validator).

Per-file changes:

* fortify_abi.rs — emit `__longjmp_chk` as a release-mode `global_asm!` symver alias that `jmp`s to host `longjmp` (avoids a Rust-frame `longjmp` that confuses the host unwinder); keep the Rust trampoline for debug builds and non-x86_64-linux. Add the matching `#[cfg]` gate on the `longjmp` extern declaration.
* io_internal_abi.rs — `bootstrap_host_libio_exports` now resolves glibc's `_IO_list_all` via `host_resolve::resolve_host_symbol_raw` instead of `dlvsym_next("GLIBC_2.2.5")`, removing one of the earliest-startup interposition reentries.
* malloc_abi.rs — `resolve_host_allocator_symbol` collapses the GLIBC_2.2.5/2.34/RTLD_NEXT cascade into a single raw-ELF call. `validate_ptr` / `check_ownership` now use the new `membrane_state::ready_pipeline()` so they return `None` (rather than panicking) when called before pipeline-ready.
* membrane_state.rs — add `ready_pipeline()` accessor: returns `Some(&ValidationPipeline)` only when state==READY and the pointer is non-null. Mirrors `try_global_pipeline` but never panics on early calls.
* pthread_abi.rs — `attr_default_stack_size` now consults `attr_stack_size_env_lookup_enabled()` before reading `FRANKENLIBC_THREAD_STACK_SIZE`, gating env access on `is_runtime_ready` AND not-in-pipeline-init AND not-in-malloc-reentry AND not-in-threading-policy AND not-in-validation-context. Promote the 2 MiB literal to `ATTR_DEFAULT_STACK_SIZE` so both paths reuse it.
* startup_abi.rs — `delegate_to_host_libc_start_main` and `host_cxa_thread_atexit_impl` resolve their host symbols via `resolve_host_symbol_raw` rather than `dlvsym_next("GLIBC_2.34"/"2.2.5"/"2.17")`. Comment explains why: calling host `dlvsym` before glibc finishes TLS/dlerror init can crash during loader startup.
* stdio_abi.rs — `ensure_host_libio_exit_safe` short-circuits when runtime not ready, then promotes the AcqRel swap to a compare_exchange so we don't burn the "patched" flag before we ever execute the patch. `init_host_stdio_streams` now guards COPY-relocation syncing in release builds on `is_runtime_ready` so we don't touch host stdio state too early.
* time_abi.rs — vDSO resolution path is now gated by `vdso_resolution_enabled()` (runtime ready AND not pipeline-initializing). When disabled, `vdso_fastpath_snapshot` still reports `mapping_present` from `getauxval(AT_SYSINFO_EHDR)` so diagnostics keep working, but `clock_gettime`/`gettimeofday` fall back to raw syscalls. Drop the unused `vdso_symbol_version_cstr` helper.
* version_scripts/libc.map — drop `__longjmp_chk` from the GLIBC_2.2.5 export list (it's now emitted as a symver via `global_asm!` in fortify_abi.rs); add the NIS / xdr / readColdStartFile / writeColdStartFile entries that the recent NIS+xdr porting commits added on the Rust side but missed on the version script (nis_addmember/creategroup/destroy_object/destroygroup/ismember/print_*/removemember/verifygroup; xdr_cback_data, xdr_domainname, xdr_keydat).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
